rulings.Case Title: DAVID BRYAN VS. U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION AS TRUSTEE FOR J.P. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Court Order To Award Undertaking To Defendant
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 1, 2015 line 1. DEFENDANT U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION AS TRUSTEE FOR J.P.'s Motion For Court Order To Award Undertaking To Defendant GRANTED. No opposition filed. Moving party prepare order. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: FRANCES SUSSNA VS. PRIME OLD COUNTY LP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order [Served Concurrently With Motion To Appoint Discovery Referee]
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 1, 2015 line 2. DEFENDANTS PRIME OLD COUNTY LP and PRIME OLD COUNTY ACQUISITIONS, LLC's Motion For Protective Order GRANTED to govern the conduct of the person Most Knowledgeable Deposition as follows: (1) Defendants must properly educate their designated PMQ before the continuation of the deposition (Maldonado v. Superior Court (2002) 94 Cal.App.4th 1390); (2) Both counsel are directed to keep colloquy to a bare minimum and avoid any personal attacks. In particular, defense counsel shall not instruct the witness not to answer any questions except on privilege. Any objections shall be limited to the legal basis for the objection (i.e. "calls for speculation") without any other commentary; (3) Plaintiff's counsel shall streamline his examination to prevent any duplicative and repetitive questioning; (4) Plaintiff is allowed a maximum of seven (7) hours to conclude the PMQ deposition. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: FRANCES SUSSNA VS. PRIME OLD COUNTY LP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Frances Sussnas Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Continued Deposition Of Prime Old County L.P. And Prime Old County Acquisition, Llcs Person Most Knowledgeable, Further Responses And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 1, 2015 line 2. PLAINTIFF FRANCES SUSSNA's Motion To Compel Continued Deposition Of Prime Old County L.P. And Prime Old County Acquisition, Llc's Person Most Knowledgeable, Further Responses And Sanctions GRANTED, in part. Person Most Knowledgeable shall appear for further deposition. Motion is denied as to the demand for production number 4 from the deposition notice. Sanctions against Defendants are granted in the amount of $2,500. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: FRANCES SUSSNA VS. PRIME OLD COUNTY LP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Appointment Of Discovery Referee; [Served Concurrently With Motion For Protective Order]
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 1, 2015 line 2. DEFENDANTS PRIME OLD COUNTY LP and PRIME OLD COUNTY ACQUISITIONS, LLC's Motion For Appointment Of Discovery Referee DENIED. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN COWDEN et al VS. PASTA POMODORO, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Allowing Amendment To First Amended Complaint To Correct Plaintiffs Names
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 1, 2015 line 3. PLAINTIFFS JOHN COWDEN and THELMA MELZER's Motion For Order Allowing Amendment To First Amended Complaint To Correct Plaintiffs Names GRANTED. The names of John Debenedetti, John Debenedetti Jr. and Frank C, Cuneo are stricken. John F. Debenedetti, Trustee of the Debenedetti Family Trust and June K Cuneo shall be named as Plaintiffs. Trial is continued to December 14, 2015. Discovery cut off is based on the new trial date for the new Plaintiffs; discovery cut-off is based on the original trial date for all other parties. Additionally, as a proper term of the amendment under CCP 473(a)(1) Plaintiff's counsel is sanctioned in the amount of $2,500 representing reasonable costs incurred by Defendant attempting to obtain discovery from deceased individuals. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ANNE EVANS ET AL VS. BONNIE MOORE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 1, 2015 line 4. CROSS DEFENDANTs ANNE EVANS and CAROLE COPLAN's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT DENIED. Request for punitive damages is sufficiently alleged. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ANNE EVANS ET AL VS. BONNIE MOORE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 1, 2015 line 4. CROSS DEFENDANT ANNE EVANS DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT OVERRULED. First Amended Cross Complaint's 4th cause of action for Fraud in the Inducement discovered in "June 2012" relates back to First Amended Complaint filed on June 8, 2015. Therefore the running of the statute of limitations is no apparent on the face of the First Amended Cross Complaint and issues of fact that cannot be properly addressed on demurrer. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: PAIGE BOGER VS. DUSTIN L WELLS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 1, 2015 line 5. DEFENDANT ANDRES BURGOS MOTION TO SET ASIDE/VACATE DEFAULT AND JUDGMENT DENIED, defendant fails to sustain burden under CCP 473(b). = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: NEIL B. GOODHUE VS. US 3 VICTUALS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 1, 2015 line 6. DEFENDANT US 3 VICTUALS, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer.
